properties.name: Bar Pass
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + standard Rajasthan geography

facts: Cuts through the central Aravalli between Ajmer and Udaipur — the historic north-south corridor of Mewar.
The Ajmer-Kishangarh-Udaipur highway (NH-8) still follows the pass's alignment.
Connects the Chappan tract of Mewar with the Godwar plain to the west.

properties.name: Piplaj Pass
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + standard Rajasthan geography

facts: Historic Aravalli pass near Ajmer used by medieval caravans between Marwar and Malwa.
Guarded a key trade route to Delhi via Ajmer through the Merwara hills.
Prithviraj Chauhan's Ajmer forces controlled this pass in the 12th c.

properties.name: Deshuri (Desuri) Pass
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + standard Rajasthan geography

facts: The main Marwar-Mewar Aravalli pass — connects Pali (Marwar) with Kumbhalgarh-Nathdwara (Mewar).
Historically the fallback escape route for Mewar rulers into Marwar during Mughal sieges.
Maharana Pratap used Deshuri to reach Chavand and Kumbhalgarh from Chittor.

properties.name: Somesar Pass
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + standard Rajasthan geography

facts: Southernmost major Aravalli pass — links Sirohi with Mount Abu and Gujarat's Banaskantha.
The highest-elevation pass in Rajasthan's Aravalli chain.
Historically used by the Deora Chauhans of Sirohi to reach their Achalgarh fort above Mount Abu.

properties.name: Jamdakol (Jilwara) Pass
properties.source: Sikhwal Manchitrawali (16th ed.) + standard Rajasthan geography

facts: Connects the Udaipur basin with Rajsamand through the Girva hills.
The Sisodia armies used Jamdakol as a strategic defile against Mughal cavalry.
Now serves the Udaipur-Nathdwara highway alignment.
